name: GenUI

unions.name: Face
unions.comment: The face of the UI cell, used for Tron UI.

unions.name: SelectKind
unions.comment: How the select switch looks and acts, used for Tron UI.

aliases.name: CellShape
aliases.comment: How many space takes the cell itself, used for Tron UI.

aliases.name: FloatDef
aliases.type: { min : Basics.Float, max : Basics.Float, step : Basics.Float, current : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: IntDef
aliases.type: { min : Basics.Int, max : Basics.Int, step : Basics.Int, current : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: NestDef
aliases.type: { children : List.List GenUI.Property, expand : Basics.Bool, nestAt : Maybe.Maybe String.String, shape : GenUI.NestShape, face : GenUI.Face }

aliases.name: NestShape
aliases.comment: How many space takes the nested panel, used for Tron UI.
aliases.type: { cols : Basics.Int, rows : Basics.Int, pages : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Path
aliases.comment:
A path to the property in the tree. So, all the first-level properties have the path of `[0]`.
The second property that lies in the folder at index `3` has a path `[0, 3, 1]`.

aliases.name: Property
aliases.type: { def : GenUI.Def, name : String.String, property : Maybe.Maybe String.String, live : Basics.Bool, shape : Maybe.Maybe GenUI.CellShape }

aliases.name: SelectDef
aliases.type: { current : String.String, values : List.List GenUI.SelectItem, nestAt : Maybe.Maybe String.String, kind : GenUI.SelectKind, shape : GenUI.NestShape }

aliases.name: SelectItem
aliases.comment: The item in the select box, a.k.a. `option`. `value` can be different from `name`, so if `name` exists, `name` should be shown to the user, but the `value` should be sent
aliases.type: { value : String.String, face : GenUI.Face, name : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

values.name: defToString
values.comment: Convert kind of the property to string, i.e. returns "int" for integer control and "xy" for XY control.

values.name: fold
values.comment: Fold the interface structure from top to bottom.

values.name: foldWithParent
values.comment: Fold the interface structure from top to bottom. The first argument is the parent property and the second one is the property being folded itselfs.
values.type: (Maybe.Maybe GenUI.Property -> GenUI.Property -> a -> a) -> a -> GenUI.GenUI -> a

values.name: foldWithPath
values.comment: Fold the interface structure from top to bottom. The function gets path and the parent property and the second argument and the third one is the property being folded itselfs.
values.type: (GenUI.Path -> Maybe.Maybe GenUI.Property -> GenUI.Property -> a -> a) -> a -> GenUI.GenUI -> a

values.name: root
values.comment:
Truly optional to use. If you want to attach the controls to some common parrent, this is the way to do it.
Don't use it anywhere in the tree except as in the root.

values.name: version
values.comment: Current version, to be accessible from code.

name: GenUI.Descriptive.Encode
comment:
Converting to Descriptive representation.

@docs Descriptive, encode, toString

name: GenUI.ToGraph
comment:
Converting to Graph.

@docs toGraph, nodeToString, edgeToString

values.name: edgeToString
values.comment: The short representation of the edge.

values.name: nodeToString
values.comment: The short representation of the property.

values.name: toGraph
values.comment:
Convert GenUI structure to Graph where nodes represent the controls and folders and edges connect child controls to the folders.

The root node has the ID of `-1`,  the ID for other nodes is calculated based on their parent and the position inside.
